title: Review: ‘Supergirl’ is a hero in search of a better movie
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Milly Alcock stars as Supergirl in a pedestrian movie directed by Craig Gillespie.
The film's blend of comedy and hero's journey is seen as derivative and uninspired.
Flashbacks exploring Kara's upbringing add depth, but the film lacks emotional impact.

title: One of Cambridge’s most beloved Japanese restaurants is closing after 34 years
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Tampopo, a Japanese restaurant in Cambridge's Porter Exchange, is closing after 34 years as owner Yasumasa Ito retires.
Owner Yasumasa Ito announced the closure in January, prompting an outpouring of memories and support from longtime patrons.
Former employee Kotaro Morita flew from Japan to assist Ito during the restaurant's final weeks before closing.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Wide receivers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots have added A.J. Brown and Romeo Doubs to their wide receiver lineup for the 2026 season.
A.J. Brown has surpassed 1,000 receiving yards in six of the last seven NFL seasons.
Quarterback Drake Maye led the league in completion percentage at 72% and average depth of target at 9.1 yards per attempt.

title: Here’s the Celtics’ reported asking price for Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics reportedly seek at least four first-round picks for trading Jaylen Brown.
Boston remains active in trade talks for Brown, following a failed bid for Giannis Antetokounmpo.
Brown is 29 and under contract for three years, coming off one of his best seasons.

title: 4 takeaways from France’s 4-1 win over Norway at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird and Kevin Slane
bullets: France defeated Norway 4-1 at Gillette Stadium, securing a first-place finish in Group I of the World Cup.
Ousmane Dembélé scored a first-half hat-trick, making it the second-fastest in World Cup history.
Erling Haaland did not play for Norway, who missed key opportunities, including a penalty saved by France's Mike Maignan.

title: When Pickles the dog saved the World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: In 1966, Pickles the dog discovered the stolen Jules Rimet Trophy, saving England's World Cup ahead of the tournament.
The trophy was stolen from Westminster Central Hall in London and later found wrapped in newspaper under a parked car.
Pickles became a celebrity and attended England's only World Cup celebration after their victory over West Germany.

title: More than 4,000 Mass General Brigham nurses to strike on July 8
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: 4,500 Mass General Brigham nurses to strike on July 8 for better wages and patient care protections.
Mass General Brigham Home Care Clinicians to start a seven-day strike on July 8 for first contract negotiations.
Hospital claims nurses are among the highest-compensated, plans five-day lockout to hire agency staff.

title: Wellesley man pleads not guilty to stabbing his father to death at playground
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: James Floyd Bennett III pleads not guilty to first-degree murder after allegedly stabbing his father at Wellesley playground.
The victim, James Floyd Bennett Jr., was found with 20 stab wounds and died at Newton-Wellesley Hospital after the attack.
Bennett III, with a history of mental health issues, turned himself in after allegedly hiding the weapon and showering.

title: Young boy hospitalized after falling from second-floor window in Worcester
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 4-year-old boy fell from a second-story window in Worcester, Massachusetts, and is expected to recover.
Worcester Police responded to a Southold Road home around 5 p.m. Thursday following the emergency call.
The child is hospitalized in stable condition with bumps and bruises, and the incident investigation is ongoing.

title: 12-year-old dead, another child injured after bus collides with motorized scooter in Haverhill
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 12-year-old girl died and another child was injured after a bus-scooter collision in Haverhill, Thursday.
The crash occurred at Portland Street and 5th Avenue; police continue to investigate, seeking witnesses.
School Superintendent Margaret Marotta urged community compassion and discussed scooter safety in response.
